title: petrea kohautiana f. alba
intro: Petrea kohautiana f. alba, also known as White Petrea, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Verbenaceae family. It is a white-flowering variant of Petrea kohautiana, a native New Zealand shrub. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in New Zealand and other Pacific Island cultures, where it is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin irritations, and are believed to offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
